questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A uniform plank of mass m, free to move in horizontal direction, is placed on the top of a solid cylinder of mass 2 m and radius R. The plank is attached with one end of a mass less spring of force constant $k$ and the other end of spring is fixed with a wall as shown in fig. The plank is shiftily displaced towards spring and released. There is no slipping anv where then
options: Total energy of the system, when compression is spring is $x$ and velocity of plank is v, is $\frac{7}{8} \mathrm{mv}^2+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{kx}^2$
Angular frequency of oscillation is $\sqrt{\frac{4 \mathrm{k}}{7 \mathrm{~m}}}$
Time period of oscillation is $\pi \sqrt{\frac{7 \mathrm{~m}}{4 \mathrm{k}}}$
Angular frequency oscillation is $\sqrt{\frac{2 k}{7 m}}$
questions.solution:
At the instant when velocity of plank is v velocity of C.M. of cylinder is $\frac{\mathrm{V}}{2}$ and angular velocity of cylinder is $\omega$
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{E}=\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{mv}^2+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{kx}^2+\frac{1}{2} 2 \mathrm{~m}\left(\frac{\mathrm{v}}{2}\right)^2+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{I}\left(\frac{\mathrm{~V} / 2}{\mathrm{R}}\right)^2 \\
& \mathrm{I}=\frac{2 \mathrm{mR}^2}{\mathrm{R}}=\mathrm{mR}^2 \\
& =\frac{7}{8} \mathrm{mv}^2+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{kx}^2
\end{aligned}$

For free oscillation $\frac{d E}{d t}=0 \Rightarrow a=-\left(\frac{4 k}{7 m}\right) x$
$\Rightarrow \omega^2=\frac{4 \mathrm{k}}{7 \mathrm{~m}} \text { and } \mathrm{t}=\frac{2 \pi}{\omega}=2 \pi \sqrt{\frac{7 \mathrm{~m}}{4 \mathrm{k}}}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A spring mass system is hanging from the ceiling of an elevator in equilibrium. The elevator suddenly starts accelerating upwards with acceleration a, consider all the options in the reference frame of elevator.
options: the frequency of oscillation is $\frac{1}{2 \pi} \sqrt{\frac{\mathrm{k}}{\mathrm{m}}}$
the amplitude of the resulting SHM is $\frac{\mathrm{ma}}{\mathrm{k}}$
amplitude of resulting SHM is $\frac{\mathrm{m}(\mathrm{g}+\mathrm{a})}{\mathrm{k}}$
maximum speed of block during oscillation is $\left(\sqrt{\frac{\mathrm{m}}{\mathrm{k}}}\right) \mathrm{a}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A particle executes simple harmonic motion between $\mathrm{x}=-\mathrm{A}$ and $\mathrm{x}=+\mathrm{A}$. The time taken for it to move from $x=0$ to $x=\frac{A}{2}$ is $T_1$ and to move from $x=\frac{A}{2}$ to $x=\frac{A}{\sqrt{2}}$ is $T_2$. Then
options: $\mathrm{T}_1 \lt \mathrm{T}_2$
$\mathrm{T}_1=\mathrm{T}_2$
$\mathrm{T}_1=2 \mathrm{~T}_2$
$\mathrm{T}_2=2 \mathrm{~T}_1$
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& x=A \sin \omega t ...(1)\\
& \frac{A}{2}=A \sin \omega T_1...(2) \\
& \frac{A}{\sqrt{2}}=A \sin \omega\left(T_1+T_2\right)
\end{aligned}$

From eq. (1), $\omega \mathrm{T}_1=\frac{\pi}{6}$
From eq. (2), $\omega\left(T_1+T_2\right)=\frac{\pi}{4}$
$\begin{aligned}
& \therefore \frac{T_1+T_2}{T_1}=\frac{6}{4} \\
& \therefore T_1=2 T_2
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Two identical simple pendulums each of length L and mass m are connected by a weightless spring as shown in figure. The force constant of the spring is k. In equilibrium the pendulums are vertical and the spring is horizontal and un-deformed. The frequency of small oscillations of the linked pendulums, when they are deflected from their equilibrikum positions through equal displacements in the same vertical plane in the same direction is $f_1$ and when displaced in opposite directions the frequency is $f_2$. Then
options: $f_1=\frac{1}{2 \pi} \sqrt{\frac{g}{2 L}}$
$f_1=\frac{1}{2 \pi} \sqrt{\frac{g}{L}}$
$f_2=\frac{1}{2 \pi} \sqrt{\frac{g}{L}+\frac{2 k}{m}}$
$f_2=\frac{1}{2 \pi} \sqrt{\frac{g}{2 L}+\frac{k}{m}}$
questions.solution:
(a) When both the pendulums are displaced in the same direction by same amount, the spring will neither compress or stretch, so the restoring torque on each pendulum about the point of suspension will be due to its own weight only.
i.e. $\tau=-m g L \sin \theta=-m g L \theta$ [ as for small $\theta, \sin \theta=\theta$ ] But by definition $\tau=l \alpha=M L^2 \frac{d^2 \theta}{d t^2}\left[\right.$ as $\left.l=m L^2\right]$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A cylindrical block of density $d$ stays fully immersed in a beaker filled with two immiscible liquids of different densities $d_1$ and $d_2$. The block is in equilibrium with half of it in liquid 1 and the other half in liquid 2. If the block is given small downward displacement, neglecting frictional loses, then
options: it executes simple harmonic motion
it motion is oscillatory but not SHM
frequency of oscillation depends on size of the cylinder
displacement of centre of cylinder is symmetric about its equilibrium position
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A long thin uniform rod of length L and mass m is free to rotate in a vertical plane about a horizontal fixed axis through its one end "O". A light spring of force constant $k$ is connected vertically between the other end A of the rod and ground. When the rod is in equilibrium, it is parallel to the ground.
options: The period of small oscillations when the rod is rotated slightly and released is $2 \pi \sqrt{\frac{m}{3 k}}$
The period of small oscillations when the rod is rotated slightly and released is $2 \pi \sqrt{\frac{m}{k}}$

The maximum speed of the displaced end of the rod is $L \theta_0 \sqrt{\frac{3 k}{m}}$
(The angular amplitude of small oscillations $I \theta_0$ )
The maximum speed of the displaced end of the rod is $L \theta_0 \sqrt{\frac{3 k}{m}}$ (The angular amplitude of small oscillations is $\theta_0$ )
questions.solution:
(a) Restoring torque about "O" due to elastic force of the spring,
$\begin{aligned}
& \tau=-F L=-k y L(F=k y) \\
& \tau=-k L^2 \theta(\text { as } y=L \theta)
\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned}
& \tau=l \alpha=\frac{1}{3} M L^2 \frac{d^2 \theta}{d t^2} \\
& \frac{1}{3} M L^2 \frac{d^2 \theta}{d t^2}=-k L^2 \theta \\
& \frac{d^2 \theta}{d t^2}=-\frac{3 k}{M} \theta \quad \text { or } \quad \omega=\sqrt{\frac{3 k}{M}} \text { and } T=2 \pi \sqrt{\frac{M}{3 K}}
\end{aligned}$
(b) In angular SHM, maximum angular velocity
$\begin{aligned}
& \left(\frac{d \theta}{d t}\right)_{\max }=\theta_0 \omega=\theta_0 \sqrt{\frac{3 k}{M}} \\
& v=r(d \theta / d t)
\end{aligned}$

So, $v_{\max }=L\left(\frac{d \theta}{d t}\right)_{\max }=L \theta_0 \sqrt{\frac{3 k}{M}}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A certain rough platform perform oscillating given by $y_p=A \sin \omega t \hat{j}$, while another block kept on the platform perform oscillations on the platform given by $\mathrm{x}_{\mathrm{B}}=\mathrm{A} \cos (\omega \mathrm{t}) \hat{\mathrm{i}}$. As seen from, the ground.
options: The direction of frictional force on the block will always be opposite to its velocity relative to the platform.
The direction of frictional force on the block will be always opposite to its relative acceleration w.r.t. platform.
The direction of frictional force on the block at any instant will be $\cos (\omega t) \hat{i}+\sin (\omega t) \hat{j}$
Direciton of friction at any instant will be $\sin (\omega t) \hat{i}+\cos (\omega t) \hat{j}$.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & v_p=A \omega \cos \omega t \hat{j} \\ & v_B=-A \omega \sin (\omega t) \hat{j} \\ & \Rightarrow \text { Direction of friction }=\cos \omega t \hat{i}+\sin \omega t \hat{j}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: An ideal spring has natural length 40 cm and spring constant $500 \mathrm{~N} / \mathrm{m}$. A block of mass 1 kg is attached at one end of the spring and other end of the spring is attached to ceiling. The block released from the position, where the spring has length $45 \mathrm{~cm} .\left(\mathrm{g}=10 \mathrm{~ms}^{-2}\right)$
options: the block will perform SHM of amplitude 5 cm .
the block will have maximum velocity $30 \sqrt{5} \mathrm{~cm} / \mathrm{sec}$
the block will have maximum acceleration $15 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$
the minimum potential energy of the spring will be zero
questions.solution:
Extension is 5 cm and equilibrium is at the extension of 2 cm so Amplitude of SHM is 3 cm
Also $\omega w=\sqrt{\frac{k}{m}}=10 \sqrt{5} \mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{s}$
$\begin{aligned} & v_{\max }=\omega \mathrm{A}=30 \sqrt{5} \mathrm{~ms}^{-1} \\ & \mathrm{a}_{\max }=\omega^2 \mathrm{~A}=15 \mathrm{~ms}^{-2}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A bob of mass $2 m$ hangs by a string attached to the block of mass $m$ of a spring block system. The whole arrangement is in a state of equilibrium. The bob of mass 2 m is pulled down slowly by a distance $x_0$ and released.
options: For $\mathrm{x}_0=\frac{3 \mathrm{mg}}{\mathrm{k}}$ maximum tension in string is 4 mg
For $\mathrm{x}_0\gt\frac{3 \mathrm{mg}}{\mathrm{k}}$, minimum tension in string is mg
Frequency of oscillation of system is $\frac{1}{2 \pi} \sqrt{\frac{\mathrm{k}}{3 \mathrm{~m}}}$, for all non-zero values of $\mathrm{x}_0$
The motion will remain simple harmonic for $\mathrm{x}_0 \leq \frac{3 \mathrm{mg}}{\mathrm{k}}$
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \text { (A) } \mathrm{kx}=3 \mathrm{mg} ; \mathrm{equ}^{\mathrm{n}} . \\ & \mathrm{k}\left(\mathrm{x}+\mathrm{x}_0\right)-\mathrm{T}-\mathrm{mg}=\mathrm{ma} \qquad ....(1)\\ & \mathrm{~T}-2 \mathrm{mg}=2 \mathrm{ma} \qquad ....(2) \end{aligned}$ On solving, $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{T}=\frac{2 \mathrm{~m}}{3} \mathrm{k}\left(\mathrm{x}+\mathrm{x}_0\right) \quad \therefore \text { for } \mathrm{x}_0=\frac{3 \mathrm{mg}}{\mathrm{k}} \\ & \mathrm{~T}=4 \mathrm{mg} \end{aligned}$ (D) If $\mathrm{x}_0=\frac{3 \mathrm{mg}}{\mathrm{k}}$ If $x_0\gt\frac{3 m g}{k}$ then string will become lack when ' $m$ ' comes to rest at top most extreme possible.]
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A ball is swinging on a swing like a simple pendulum. Its time period of oscillation is T and amplitude is A . When it is at the bottom of the swing, another ball of equal mass strikes and sticks to it while both of them are travelling in same direction. Choose the correct option(s).
options: The time period of oscillation remains same.
The amplitude increases.
The time period of oscillation increases.
The time period of oscillation decreases.
questions.solution: Since time period of oscillation is indpenedent of mass of bob, thus remains same. Due to collision, K.E. at the mean position increases $\Rightarrow$ amplitude increases.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: The system shown is hanging in equilibrium well above the ground. When the string is cut at $\mathrm{t}=0$, the time at which the spring is at its natural length will be (Take $\pi^2=10$ )
options: $\frac{1}{6} \mathrm{~s}$
$\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{~s}$
$\frac{1}{3} \mathrm{~s}$
$\frac{2}{3} \mathrm{~s}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A body is performing S.H.M., then its
options: average total energy per cycle is equal to its maximum kinetic energy.
average kinetic energy per cycle is equal to half of its maximum kinetic energy.
mean velocity over a complete cycle is equal to $2 / \pi$ times of its maximum velocity.
root mean square velocity is $1 / \sqrt{ } 2$ times of its maximum velocity
questions.solution: $v_{m m s}=\sqrt{\frac{v_0^2 \int_0^T \cos ^2(\omega t+\phi) d t}{\int_0^T d T}}=\frac{v_0}{\sqrt{2}}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Consider a spring that exerts the following restoring force
$\begin{array}{ll}
F=-k x & \text { for } x\gt0 \\
F=-4 k x & \text { for } x \lt 0
\end{array}$
A mass $m$ on a frictionless surface is attached to the spring displaced to $x=A$ by stretching the spring and released :
options: The period of motion will be $\mathrm{T}=\frac{3}{2} \pi \sqrt{\frac{m}{k}}$
the most negative value the mass m can reach will be $\mathrm{x}=-\frac{A}{2}$
The time taken to move from $\mathrm{x}=\mathrm{A}$ to $\mathrm{x}=-\frac{A}{\sqrt{2}}$, straight away will be equal to $\frac{5 \pi}{8} \sqrt{\frac{m}{k}}$
The total energy of oscillations will be $\frac{5}{2} k A^2$
questions.solution:
For (A) : $T=\frac{T_1}{2}+\frac{T_2}{2}=\pi \sqrt{\frac{m}{k}}+\pi \sqrt{\frac{m}{4 k}}=\frac{3 \pi}{2} \sqrt{\frac{m}{k}}$
For (B) $\frac{1}{2} k(A)^2=\frac{1}{2} 4 k(x)^2$
For (C) : Not possible $\quad\left[x=\frac{-A}{2}\right]$
For (D): $\mathrm{TE}=\frac{1}{2} k A^2$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
